destinations.evidence: After heartbeat revival transactions are synthesized, the builder checks the same global deadline before fulfilled-async collection, delivery collection, sequencing-rights reorder, and transaction execution, skipping or breaking when elapsed.

equivalence_argument: The port enforces the global payload build deadline as a phase gate. If heartbeat revival consumes the budget, subsequent collection, sorting, and execution phases are skipped or short-circuited for that attempt.
notes: The checks are distributed across phase boundaries rather than a single post-revival return.
rationale: The diff introduces a global deadline and an immediate post-checkAndRevive guard that logs and skips downstream phases when the deadline has elapsed. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If the global payload build deadline is already exceeded after heartbeat revival, the builder skips the remaining collection, sorting, and execution phases for that attempt.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure a very small build deadline and force heartbeat revival to consume it; assert later payload collection/sort/execution phases do not run in that attempt.
summary: The payload build deadline gates later phases.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Configure a tiny build_deadline, make heartbeat revival consume it, and assert fulfilled/delivery collection, sequencing-rights reorder, and candidate execution do not run after the deadline.
title: Build deadline can short-circuit later phases
